title: Ophrys panormitana
taxonomy: Ophrys panormitana belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This species is part of the genus Ophrys, which includes orchids that mimic the appearance of female insects to attract pollinators. Within the genus Ophrys, Ophrys panormitana is classified as a type of bee orchid, known for its unique flowers that resemble female bees. The species Ophrys panormitana is native to the island of Sicily in Italy. It is a type of terrestrial orchid, growing in a variety of environments.
